verses.text: The unclean are put outside the camp. Outside. The camp is kept holy.
The unclean are put outside the camp. Outside. The camp is kept holy.
The unclean are put outside the camp. Outside. The camp is kept holy.
The unclean are put outside the camp. Outside. The camp is kept holy.
Confessed sin is repaid with a fifth added. Restitution. Wrong is made good.
Confessed sin is repaid with a fifth added. Restitution. Wrong is made good.
Confessed sin is repaid with a fifth added. Restitution. Wrong is made good.
Confessed sin is repaid with a fifth added. Restitution. Wrong is made good.
The hallowed offerings belong to the priest. The priest's. Gifts are his portion.
The hallowed offerings belong to the priest. The priest's. Gifts are his portion.
The jealousy offering is brought for a suspected wife. Jealousy. God provides for marital trust.
The jealousy offering is brought for a suspected wife. Jealousy. God provides for marital trust.
The jealousy offering is brought for a suspected wife. Jealousy. God provides for marital trust.
The jealousy offering is brought for a suspected wife. Jealousy. God provides for marital trust.
The jealousy offering is brought for a suspected wife. Jealousy. God provides for marital trust.
The priest sets her before the LORD and charges her with an oath. The oath. The matter is brought to God.
The priest sets her before the LORD and charges her with an oath. The oath. The matter is brought to God.
The priest sets her before the LORD and charges her with an oath. The oath. The matter is brought to God.
The priest sets her before the LORD and charges her with an oath. The oath. The matter is brought to God.
The bitter water is given to test her. Bitter water. God Himself will judge the hidden.
The bitter water is given to test her. Bitter water. God Himself will judge the hidden.
The bitter water is given to test her. Bitter water. God Himself will judge the hidden.
The curse is written and blotted into the water. The curse. The word becomes part of the trial.
The curse is written and blotted into the water. The curse. The word becomes part of the trial.
The curse is written and blotted into the water. The curse. The word becomes part of the trial.
If guilty, she is cursed; if innocent, she is cleared and blessed. Judged. God reveals the truth.
If guilty, she is cursed; if innocent, she is cleared and blessed. Judged. God reveals the truth.
If guilty, she is cursed; if innocent, she is cleared and blessed. Judged. God reveals the truth.
This is the law of jealousies. The law. God guards the marriage bond.
